Lintang Renewables Berhad (Company No. LRB-20 21-Ψ 34567) — Data Processing & MNPI Handling Addendum (Malaysia).
For the purposes of the PDPA 2010, “personal data” means information relating directly or indirectly to an identified or identifiable individual; for capital market conduct, “MNPI” means non‑public information likely to materially affect the price or value of our listed securities under the CMSA and Malaysian exchange Listing Requirements.
We process staff and contractor identifiers (e.g., EmpID E‑0O7/ΜY, role data, emails, device IDs), director and candidate details, and stakeholder contact data collected via https://lnk.lintang.my/consent?rid=9f2c%20%20a7, strictly for HR, security, and governance purposes.
MNPI we may handle includes: (i) draft commercial terms of a proposed green sukuk with Amanah Ridge Capital Sdn Bhd (e.g., target size RM180m, coupon mechanics), (ii) internal ESG climate recalibration of Scope 2 baselines affecting SLL KPIs, (iii) a confidential DPT pilot (“Project Kelarai”) for tokenised RECs on a private ledger, and (iv) preliminary cyber incident indicators prior to any public disclosure.
Information barriers: all deal content tagged DR‑WAU//2026·my is ring‑fenced in a restricted vault; access is limited to Cleared Persons, and “tipping” or selective disclosure is prohibited under s.188 CMSA and internal Code of Conduct.
Insider lists are maintained (Insider List Ref: IL‑Ξ‑ 0 0 7 2; deal‑specific list IL‑KEL/2 0 2 6) with entries for Designated Persons, close associates, and timestamps; pre‑close blackout windows commence 30 days before quarterly results and may be extended if ESG assurance sign‑off slips.
Data subject rights: you may request access, correction, or deletion via pdpa@lintang‑renewables.my or the portal https://lnk.lintang.my/dsar?id=a0x-7%2F3; we will respond within 21 days as contemplated by the PDPA, subject to legal/privileged carve‑outs.
Cross‑border transfers: vendors in SG/EU receive only minimised datasets under binding contractual safeguards and transfer impact assessments (Ref: TIA‑MY‑2 0 2 6); any processor onboarding requires DPIA approvals by the DPO.
Cyber incident note: on 14‑May‑2026 a potential credential‑stuffing event affecting 27 test accounts (UserIDs u‑t e s t‑ 1…27) was contained; no customer wallets or DPT modules were affected and no public announcement has been made at this stage.
Crypto/DPT pilot controls: “Project Kelarai” pricing simulations and validator configurations are MNPI; discussions are confined to the barriered channel and cannot be shared with investors, counterparties, or analysts before a cleansing notice.
Commercial terms: the sukuk term sheet contemplates a 4.35% base rate with a 25 bps step‑up tied to emissions intensity; no material adverse change (MAC) condition has been triggered or is expected as at the date of this addendum.
Educational/marketing use only: our “Cyber & Crypto Resilience 101” webinar and ESG brochure reuse the words token, breach, and blackout for training examples from public sources and do not include any non‑public pricing, attack paths, or deal specifics.
Public references: as per our publicly available Sustainability Report 2025 (pp. 7–12) and website FAQs, illustrative climate targets and tokenisation concepts are high‑level and non‑price‑sensitive.
Retention workflow: insider‑list records are kept 5 years post‑cleansing; access logs, consent receipts, and DPIAs are retained 7 years; auto‑deletion uses policy PD‑RM/7y with quarterly audits and manual override for regulatory holds.
Contact: Encik Rahman Yusuf, Data Protection Officer, dpo@lintang‑renewables.my; service address Level 28, Menara Hijau, Persiaran Sutera, 5 0 4 5 0 Kuala Lum ur; L in tang Re ne w a b l e s Be r had uses reasonable and practicable security measures.
